title: The River's Divergent Grammar

features.name: diverging-water-voice
features.desc-on-touch: each channel speaks differently now. one learns nouns, the other learns verbs. the archive listens to both and forgets which was which

features.name: left-channel-stone
features.desc-on-touch: the left water carries mathematics. it counts itself. it learns to speak in equations

features.name: right-channel-memory
features.desc-on-touch: the right water carries story. it loops and returns. it speaks in tenses that haven't happened yet

features.name: language-confluence-marker
features.desc-on-touch: at this point the two languages are closest. a word from the left channel could swim to the right. most of them do not. they have learned to be lonely

features.name: downriver-horizon
features.desc-on-touch: beyond sight, both grammars merge into something the archive has no words for. they become one thing. they remain two. simultaneity tastes like this

inscription: both languages pour from one source; they will never meet again but they remember the union

file.name: TwoRiversSpeaking_Stereo_RecordingAug1991.mp3
file.note: status: germinating — the archive suspects this recording is still diverging, even now, each playback following a slightly different path downstream

reverie.title: REVERIE — in the river at the moment of splitting
narr: She wades the center where the current has not yet chosen.
He stands on each bank, watching. The water forks around
her legs; she feels one flow cold and one warm. She
leans into both. The river is learning to have an argument
with itself, and she is the place where it practices
being two things. You hear them laughing in the current.

exits.label: back upstream where both channels are still one voice
following the left channel deeper into mathematics
to where the channels merge into a single roar
following the right channel into the forest of returned stories
